introSection: Jester was a steel backwards roller coaster previously located at Six Flags New Orleans in New Orleans, Louisiana, USA. The ride was built by Vekoma. It originally operated at Six Flags Fiesta Texas as Joker's Revenge from 1996 to 2001.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
On May 10, 1996, Joker's Revenge opened to the public. It had a funhouse queue line, as well as a chicken exit in the station that guests could use if they chickened out during the final moments before boarding the ride.

Joker's Revenge was closed in 2001 and did not operate at all during the 2002 season. The ride was dismantled at the end of Summer 2002. The funhouse remained until Summer 2006 when it was replaced by Pandemonium. The queue bridge was also reused for Pandemonium.

The ride was moved to Six Flags New Orleans, where it opened on April 13, 2003 as Jester.

The attraction was closed with the park just before Hurricane Katrina struck on August 26, 2005. The ride was damaged beyond repair and unsalvageable due to the park being flooded with 8 feet of water. It remained standing but not operating until it was demolished in late 2024.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: As the train rolled out of the station, riders were pulled 79 feet up the chain lift hill. Riders then dropped 69 feet at 40 mph. After the drop, riders approached a vertical loop. This was followed by a double corkscrew and a helix. The train then hit the final brake run and returned to the station.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Green track and blue supports. The ride featured magenta track and supports when it operated at Fiesta Texas.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Riders were held in with over-the-shoulder harnesses. The trains where build by Arrow Dynamics.

sections.title: External links
sections.content:
* Jester on Six Flags New Orleans website
 * Joker's Revenge on Six Flags Fiesta Texas website

fullText:
Jester was a steel backwards roller coaster previously located at Six Flags New Orleans in New Orleans, Louisiana, USA. The ride was built by Vekoma. It originally operated at Six Flags Fiesta Texas as Joker's Revenge from 1996 to 2001.

On May 10, 1996, Joker's Revenge opened to the public. It had a funhouse queue line, as well as a chicken exit in the station that guests could use if they chickened out during the final moments before boarding the ride.

Joker's Revenge was closed in 2001 and did not operate at all during the 2002 season. The ride was dismantled at the end of Summer 2002. The funhouse remained until Summer 2006 when it was replaced by Pandemonium. The queue bridge was also reused for Pandemonium.

The ride was moved to Six Flags New Orleans, where it opened on April 13, 2003 as Jester.

The attraction was closed with the park just before Hurricane Katrina struck on August 26, 2005. The ride was damaged beyond repair and unsalvageable due to the park being flooded with 8 feet of water. It remained standing but not operating until it was demolished in late 2024.



As the train rolled out of the station, riders were pulled 79 feet up the chain lift hill. Riders then dropped 69 feet at 40 mph. After the drop, riders approached a vertical loop. This was followed by a double corkscrew and a helix. The train then hit the final brake run and returned to the station.

Green track and blue supports. The ride featured magenta track and supports when it operated at Fiesta Texas.

Riders were held in with over-the-shoulder harnesses. The trains where build by Arrow Dynamics.




 * Jester on Six Flags New Orleans website
 * Joker's Revenge on Six Flags Fiesta Texas website
